item.title: Olivia wins USA's first women's weightlifting gold medal in 24 years :)
item.description: Olivia Reeves hoisted the United States to its first #Olympic weightlifting gold medal in 24 years on Thursday, winning the women's 71kg (157 pounds) competition. https://sports.yahoo.com/paris-olympics-olivia-reeves-wins-usas-first-womens-weightlifting-gold-medal-in-24-years-202816919.html #unmuted

item.title: He's the right type
item.description: I need one of these. It's out of CTRL. I hope you smile or crack a mental smirk at some point today, from this or an unrelated thing - even if only a brief moment. Cheers!!
item.dc:creator: epicurusaquinas

item.title: Bro let his adopted dog choose name she likes
item.dc:creator: TheWhatever666

item.title: Anyone else absolutely hate these algorithms on other social media platforms?
item.description: Like just because I watched a video of someone trimming the hooves of a cow doesn’t mean I’m a ranch hand living in Montana looking for tips on how to improve my hoof trimming technique lol. Any time I click on anything on any other social media app, my suggestions go nuts and I have to constantly click on “not interested” or whatever. These algorithms for suggestions are supposed to drive engagement but all it does is annoy the shit out of me. I like Imgur just being random, usersub is great, I can watch whatever nonsense video and the app doesn’t assume I’m a connoisseur of that thing.
item.dc:creator: CapnAdorable

item.title: Tonight the entire women's basketball team of the local university strolled into the bar at my favorite Mexican restaurant, all dressed to kill... and yeah, I just sorta died.
item.description:
Forgot all about that guac, not gonna lie. I pretty much keeled right over. My wife, who's 5'11", just rolled her eyes and threw tortilla chips at me.
*sighhhhhhh*

item.title: Faraday had no formal math education and worked all these out by observation and graphs
item.description: Faraday never published any grant mathematical theorems like Maxwell, but his observation and graphs were so on point, even today, introduction to electromagnetism starts with his work.

item.title: The moderator we need in a political debate
item.description:
"present" should be president I believe
Politicians serve the people not the other way around. Kyle Clark should moderate all political debates. He sets the powerbalance straight and by that returns the power back to its rightful holders, the citizens, the voters. A tictok reactionvideo I know... The whole piece https://x.com/Canaansdad1987/status/1822227308146487317
item.dc:creator: nickontop
